Season Simulation

Source: vignettes/season-simulation.Rmd
season-simulation.Rmd

Overview

The simulate_season() function simulates remaining AFL matches based on team ratings and fixture data. Run many simulations to generate probabilistic forecasts for finals qualification, ladder positions, and premiership chances.

How It Works

For each unplayed match, the simulation:

  1. Calculates an expected margin from the team TORP rating difference
  2. Adds home ground advantage (SIM_HOME_ADVANTAGE = 6 points)
  3. Adds random noise (SIM_NOISE_SD = 26 points) to reflect game-to-game variance
  4. Records the simulated result

Completed matches keep their actual results.

Running a Simulation 

library(torp)

# simulate_season() requires:
# - sim_teams: data frame with team ratings (one row per team)
# - sim_games: fixture data with NA results for unplayed games
sim_result <- simulate_season(sim_teams, sim_games)

Multiple Simulations

For probabilistic forecasts, run many simulations and aggregate:

n_sims <- 1000

all_sims <- lapply(seq_len(n_sims), function(i) {
  sim <- simulate_season(sim_teams, sim_games)
  sim$sim_id <- i
  sim
})

all_sims_df <- data.table::rbindlist(all_sims)

Analysing Results 

library(dplyr)

# Finals probability by team
finals_prob <- all_sims_df |>
  group_by(sim_id) |>
  arrange(desc(wins), desc(percentage)) |>
  mutate(ladder_pos = row_number()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  group_by(team) |>
  summarise(
    top_4_pct = mean(ladder_pos <= 4) * 100,
    top_8_pct = mean(ladder_pos <= 8) * 100,
    avg_wins = mean(wins)
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(top_8_pct))

finals_prob

Key Parameters

The simulation uses constants defined in R/constants.R:

Constant Default Description
SIM_NOISE_SD 26 Standard deviation of random margin noise
SIM_HOME_ADVANTAGE 6 Points added to home team’s expected margin
SIM_WP_SCALING_FACTOR 50 Scaling factor for converting TORP diff to win probability

See Also

  • vignette("player-ratings") – TORP rating methodology (drives team ratings)
  • vignette("getting-started") – Installation and data loading
